2D.verdict: WORKS — Z1+Z2 is sufficient for cross-domain transfer

domain_projections.elliptic_curves: sees Z1 (conductor) + Z2 (torsion). Torsion maps PERFECTLY to Arithmos phoneme (R^2=1.0).
domain_projections.number_fields: sees Z1 (discriminant) + Z2 (class number, R^2=0.84) + Z5 (regulator, R^2=1.0). Richest projection — sees 3 of 5 latent dims.
domain_projections.genus2: sees Z1 (conductor) + Z3 (Selmer rank, maps to Bathos R^2=1.0). Selmer projects onto a DIFFERENT latent axis than torsion!
domain_projections.modular_forms: sees Z1 only (level). No independent arithmetic structure. This is why MF had zero residual.

key_finding.minimum_dims: The latent object is EXACTLY 2-dimensional for cross-domain transfer (Z1+Z2). Adding more dims does not help.
key_finding.projection_asymmetry: NF sees 3 dims, EC sees 2, G2 sees 2 (different ones!), MF sees 1. Transfer is directional because domains see different subsets.
key_finding.selmer_surprise: Genus2 Selmer rank maps to BATHOS (Z3), not ARITHMOS (Z2). It measures depth/rank, not torsion structure. The two arithmetic-looking invariants live on DIFFERENT latent axes.
